name: traffic-analysis
description: Analyze network traffic patterns, identify top applications and bandwidth usage, and optimize traffic management. Perform DPI-based traffic classification.
capabilities: Identify applications in use with DPI classification
Analyze bandwidth usage by client and application
Identify heavy users and bandwidth-intensive applications
Recommend traffic optimization and QoS policies
Track traffic patterns and trends over time
author: DataKnifeAI
securitySummary: All static findings are false positives. The skill is legitimate network traffic analysis tooling for UniFi networks. Detected patterns (URL in metadata, markdown backticks in docs, JSON field names) were incorrectly flagged by the scanner. No malicious code, command injection, or data exfiltration patterns exist. The skill performs read-only network monitoring via standard API calls.
valueStatement: Network administrators struggle to identify bandwidth bottlenecks and application usage without deep visibility. This skill provides DPI-based traffic classification and bandwidth analysis to optimize network performance and plan capacity.
userTitle: Analyze Network Traffic Patterns and Bandwidth
limitations: Requires UniFi Network API access credentials
Limited to networks using UniFi infrastructure
Historical data availability depends on controller retention settings

useCases.title: Bandwidth Troubleshooting
useCases.description: Identify which applications or clients are consuming excessive bandwidth and causing network congestion.

useCases.title: Capacity Planning
useCases.description: Analyze traffic trends to plan bandwidth upgrades and justify infrastructure investments.

useCases.title: Policy Optimization
useCases.description: Configure QoS policies based on actual application usage patterns and traffic priorities.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Traffic Overview
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me the current bandwidth usage and identify the top 10 bandwidth-consuming clients on the network.
promptTemplates.scenario: Get a quick summary of network bandwidth consumption

promptTemplates.title: Application Analysis
promptTemplates.prompt: What applications are using the most bandwidth? Break down traffic by DPI category.
promptTemplates.scenario: Understand what types of traffic dominate the network

promptTemplates.title: Congestion Investigation
promptTemplates.prompt: Analyze the network for congestion. Which clients and applications are causing the most strain?

promptTemplates.title: QoS Recommendations
promptTemplates.prompt: Based on current traffic patterns, recommend QoS policies to prioritize critical applications.

bestPractices: Monitor traffic patterns regularly to establish baseline usage
Correlate DPI categories with application policies for effective QoS
Use traffic data to justify bandwidth upgrades to stakeholders
antiPatterns: Ignoring historical trends and only reacting to immediate issues
Setting QoS rules without first understanding actual traffic patterns
Failing to document traffic policies and rationale for future reference

faq.question: What is DPI classification?
faq.answer: DPI (Deep Packet Inspection) examines network traffic beyond port numbers to identify actual applications and services based on packet content.

faq.question: Which UniFi devices are supported?
faq.answer: Any UniFi device managed by a UniFi Network Controller with API access enabled is supported.

faq.question: Can this skill block traffic?
faq.answer: No. This skill provides read-only analysis. It can recommend policies but cannot modify network configuration.

faq.question: How historical data does it access?
faq.answer: Historical data depends on your UniFi Controller retention settings. The skill accesses what the API provides.

faq.question: Is real-time monitoring available?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill can query current bandwidth metrics and traffic classifications in real-time through the API.

faq.question: What credentials are required?
faq.answer: UniFi Network Controller credentials with API access. The skill requires read-only network monitoring permissions.
